zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Hurst於大約1548年出生在英國奧可福爾可姆時，她的父親Richard Covert VII71歲，母親Anne Hendley28歲。她於大約1530年與Sir William Edmund Barttlett在英國英格蘭素可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1604年9月9日在英國英格蘭素可易阿利享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Hurst于大约1548年出生在英国奧可福爾可姆时，她的父亲Richard Covert VII71岁，母亲Anne Hendley28岁。她于大约1530年与Sir William Edmund Barttlett在英国英格兰素可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1604年9月9日在英国英格兰素可易阿利享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hurst
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived near a wood or wooded hill, from Middle English hirst(e), herst(e), hurst(e) (Old English hyrst) or a habitational name from any of the places so called, such as Hurst Green (in Mitton, Lancashire), Hirst (Northumberland), Hurst (Berkshire, Kent, Warwickshire), Hurstpierpoint (Sussex), or Hirst in Longwood (Yorkshire).
Irish: re-Anglicized form of de Horsaigh, the Gaelicized form of the English habitational name Horsey , established in Ireland since the 13th century.
German and Swiss German (also Hürst): topographic name from Middle High German hurst ‘woodland, thicket’; or a habitational name from a place so named in Westphalia.

variants.name: Herst
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or nickname from Polish herszt ‘ringleader, chieftain’.

variants.name: Horsey
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from Horsey Pignes (Somerset), Horse Eye (Sussex), or Horsey (Norfolk), from Old English hors ‘horse’ + ēg ‘island, low-lying land’.

variants.name: Hearst
meanings: Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of some similar (like-sounding) original Ashkenazic surname such as   Hirsch  .

variants.name: Horst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Phares, Alois, Armin, Fritz, Hans, Inge, Manfred, Monika, Reinhold.

North German and Dutch: topographic name from Middle Low German hurst, Middle Dutch horst ‘undergrowth, brushwood, wild place’. More specifically, the term was also used to denote a crow's or similar large bird's nest, a raised area in surrounding marshland, or an area of uncleared woodland, all of which meanings could have contributed to the surname. This surname is also a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. Compare Hurst .

History: As a name of German origin (see 1 above) the surname Horst is established among the American Mennonites. Their progenitors were the brothers Joseph, Michael, and Peter Horst, who came to PA in 1731 together with their widowed mother. Among their descendants the altered form Hurst is also found.

variants.name: Hirst
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hurst  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Hurst was born about 1548, in Combe, Oxfordshire, England, her father, Richard Covert VII, was 71 and her mother, Anne Hendley, was 28. She married Sir William Edmund Barttlett about 1530, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 9 September 1604, in Earnley, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 57.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Hurst(이)가 대략 1548년 영국 스퍼드실르 코브에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Covert VII(은)는 71세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Hendley(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 대략 1530년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 Sir William Edmund Barttlett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1604년 9월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 에른리에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Hurst родилась около 1548 г. в местности комб, оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Covert VII было 71 год, а матери, которую звали Anne Hendley, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Edmund Barttlett около 1530 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 9 сентября 1604 г. в местности эарнли, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет.
